lenses.guidance: vv 6-9 — Bezalel makes the kapporet with two cherubim guarding above. The pattern of guarded access to atonement runs through the tabernacle; the torn-veil of Mt 27:51 announces the guard removed, the seat opened to all.

The mercy seat of vv 6-9 is Hebrew kapporet; the LXX renders it hilasterion. Paul takes hilasterion and uses it of Christ in Rom 3:25 — the gold-cover-over-the-broken-law is the gospel-shape, and Christ Jesus is the substance.

Five furniture pieces — ark, table, lampstand, incense altar, anointing oil — are crafted in narrative parallel. The 'he made' refrain (vv 1, 10, 17, 25, 29) marks each item's completion before moving to the next item.
